title: If you want to cut your screen time, just get a Brick
summary: After years of struggling to curb my screen time, apparently all I needed was a $59 hunk of plastic.
myth_angle: The idea that a single device like Brick can significantly reduce screen time without additional effort or changes in behavior is overly simplistic and may not be effective for everyone.
verify_reason: The snippets show mixed results, with some users reporting a reduction in screen time and others indicating that simply getting a Brick is not enough to curb screen time, suggesting the claim may be an oversimplification.

title: Wireless charging wastes way more electricity than wired, here's why
summary: It's way more convenient, but way less efficient.
claim: Wireless charging wastes way more electricity than wired
myth_angle: The efficiency difference between wireless and wired charging may not be as significant in real-world usage as claimed, and depends on factors like charger quality and alignment
verify_reason: The snippets show that wireless charging is indeed less efficient than wired charging, with estimates of 47% to 75% more energy wasted, but the claim's significance and practical impact may be exaggerated or dependent on various factors.
